rationale: The actual thread shows only a maintainer tagging colleagues for review ("PTAL"), i.e. an escalation with no confirmed resolution — yet both candidates confidently assert a merged fix with near-identical file/line citations (same guard function, same runner line numbers), which is plausible if real but unverifiable from the given thread. X overreaches by declaring 'Status: fixed' and 'Closing as resolved,' contradicting the thread's still-open/escalated state. Y is better calibrated: it marks disposition 'keep-open,' asks the reporter to rebase and report back if the issue persists, and additionally traces the root mechanism through actual upstream vLLM files (input_processor.py:323, sampling_params.py:639, builtin.py:195/218) plus a runnable CPU-safe repro script, giving it an edge in grounding and appropriateness despite both sharing the same core diagnosis.
